description.task: Implement a CircularLinkedList class where the last node's next points back to the head. Support add, contains, and safe traversal (print a fixed number of steps to avoid infinite loop).
instructions: add(val): traverse until node.next==head (last node), set node.next=new Node(val), newNode.next=head
contains(val): set curr=head; loop size times; if curr.val==val return true; curr=curr.next; return false
print(): traverse size nodes, build string 'v1 -> v2 -> v3 -> (back to head)'
The circular property: last node's next == head (confirmed by print suffix)
Size must be maintained accurately for safe traversal
description.hint: add: Node curr=head; while(curr.next!=head) curr=curr.next; curr.next=n; n.next=head; print: use a loop for(int i=0;i<size;i++) to collect vals, build string ending with ' -> (back to head)'
description.expectedOutput:
1 -> 2 -> 3 -> (back to head)
Contains 2: true
Contains 5: false

description.task: Implement a Josephus class that uses a circular linked list to simulate the Josephus problem: n people stand in a circle, every k-th person is eliminated. Return the position (1-based) of the last person standing.
instructions: The while loop runs n-1 times (eliminates n-1 people)
Advance k-1 steps from curr: for(int i=0;i<k-1;i++) curr=curr.next
Remove k-th node: Node toRemove=curr.next; curr.next=toRemove.next; curr=curr.next
Proceed with curr pointing to the node after the removed one
description.hint: Inside while loop: for(int i=0;i<k-1;i++) curr=curr.next; curr.next=curr.next.next; curr=curr.next; remaining--;

description.task: Implement a CircularBuffer class that uses a circular linked list of fixed capacity to store integers. Support enqueue (overwrite oldest when full) and dequeue operations. Track head (oldest) and tail (newest) pointers.
instructions: enqueue when not full: buffer[tail]=val; tail=(tail+1)%capacity; size++
dequeue: int val=buffer[head]; head=(head+1)%capacity; size--; return val
The full-buffer case (overwrite) is already implemented
After enqueue(1,2,3): buffer is [1,2,3]; after enqueue(4): oldest (1) overwritten → [2,3,4]
After dequeue() from [2,3,4]: returns 2, buffer becomes [3,4]
description.hint: enqueue (non-full): buffer[tail]=val; tail=(tail+1)%capacity; size++. dequeue: int v=buffer[head]; head=(head+1)%capacity; size--; return v;
